title: 588M
description:
'

 

The adoption of the IRC rule at both Club racing level and by some of the more legendary offshore events has sparked strong interest in boats that fair well under this single number rating system.  As a result of this interest Farr Yacht Design received several commissions in 2004 for IRC oriented boats that allowed the team to embark on research work to understand the rule in some depth.  While specific formulas used to determine the IRC ratings are not published
shortSummary: A quality Racer/Cruiser that could cope with the demands of real cruising and be a strong contender for handicap wins in both round the buoys and long distance events using IRC ratings as well as PHRF and Americap.

description:
'

 

The adoption of the IRC rule at both Club racing level and by some of the more legendary offshore events has sparked strong interest in boats that fair well under this single number rating system.  As a result of this interest Farr Yacht Design received several commissions in 2004 for IRC oriented boats that allowed the team to embark on research work to understand the rule in some depth.  While specific formulas used to determine the IRC ratings are not published
shortSummary: A quality cruiser/racer that can cope with the demands of real offshore cruising and be a contender for handicap wins in both round the buoys and long distance events using the IRC rule.

description:
'

 

BENETEAU have enjoyed considerable success in the past providing sailing enthusiasts with boats that merge racing and cruising needs in one tidy economic package. Design 543 was conceived to continue providing that product in an updated and advanced form for a worldwide market place.

 

The adoption of the IRC rule at club racing level and by some of the more legendary offshore events has sparked strong interest in boats that fair well under this single number rating system.  The brief called for the boat to be competitive under IRC for club racing worldwide. BENETEAU and Farr Yacht Design undertook the challenges that this brief presented in terms of both design and production manufacturing.

 

While specific formulas used to determine the IRC ratings are not published
shortSummary: 'Design 543 will provide the club racer with scintillating sailing manners

Farr® 36 Features

 

Stylish One Design Racer with no rule influence

IRC   Yes       PHRF   Yes   IMS   No         IRM   No

Owner Driver Rule

Sail restrictions and crew weight controls

Extremely fast/high stability

No runners – masthead asymmetric spinnaker on retractable sprit

Non-overlapping jibs on athwartships tracks

ORC Category 2 legal for offshore racing (excluding safety gear

Design 491 is poised to be a giant-killer on the Mediterranean IMS circuit.  This design was commissioned by a Greek owner with the goal of winning in the large boat class.  At 43’ LOA she will be one of the smallest boats in her class
shortSummary: Design 491 is poised to be a giant-killer on the Mediterranean IMS circuit. While this design is sized to be at the small end of the big boat class it will be fast enough to sail in front of the 45’ cruiser/racers.
